claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're asking about.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you're asking about?
- Their names (or you can use Child 1, Child 2, etc. if you prefer)?
- Their ages?

This will help me guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I have one child - Alex, who is 12 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Alex is 12 years old and lives with you in the UK - that's helpful.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Are you and Alex usually living in the UK?** In other words, is the UK your main home where you normally live?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. We both live in the UK - it's our main home where we normally live.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to ask about your immigration status:

**Do you have the right to live in the UK?** This means things like:
- Being a British citizen, or
- Having settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or
- Having pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or
- Having another type of valid immigration status that allows you to claim benefits

What is your situation regarding your right to live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have the right to live in the UK. I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask about Alex specifically:

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?**

To clarify:
- Non-advanced education means education up to A levels, Scottish Highers, or equivalent (not university degree level)
- This would include things like secondary school, sixth form, or college courses below degree level

Since Alex is 12, I imagine they're in secondary school, but I just want to confirm their education status.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in secondary school - year 8. So yes, they're in full-time education.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask an important question about who Alex lives with:

**Does Alex live with you, or do they live with someone else (like another parent, a relative, or in care)?**

You mentioned earlier that Alex lives with you, but I want to make sure there isn't a situation where Alex might be living with someone else or splitting time between homes. For example:
- Does Alex live with you full-time?
- Or do they split their time between you and another parent or guardian?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me full-time. They don't split time between homes or live with anyone else.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Just a couple more questions:

**Is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care (like a children's home or foster care)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or in any kind of local authority care. They're just at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing all that information.

Let me now ask one final question to complete the assessment:

**Are you the person who is primarily responsible for Alex?** In other words, are you the main person who looks after Alex and makes decisions about their upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I'm the main person responsible for Alex - I look after them and make all the decisions about their care and upbringing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on all the information you've provided, you meet all the requirements:

✓ **Alex qualifies** - They are 12 years old (under 16) and in full-time education (year 8 secondary school)

✓ **You're responsible** - You are the main person responsible for Alex, looking after them and making decisions about their upbringing

✓ **Living arrangements** - Alex lives with you full-time in the UK, which is your main home

✓ **Immigration status** - You're a British citizen, so you have the right to live in the UK and claim benefits

✓ **No special circumstances** - Alex is not in hospital or local authority care

You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. The benefit is paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or getting certain other benefits), and the current rates are paid for each child you're responsible for.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

reasonings: Alex is 12 years old, which is under 16, so they qualify as a child for Child Benefit purposes. Alex is in full-time non-advanced education (year 8 secondary school). Alex lives with the claimant full-time in the UK, which is their main home. The claimant is a British citizen and therefore has the right to live in the UK. The claimant is the person primarily responsible for Alex, looking after them and making decisions about their upbringing. Alex is not in hospital or local authority care. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with their 12-year-old child Alex, who is in full-time secondary education. The claimant is the primary person responsible for Alex, who lives with them full-time and is not in hospital or care. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.

The claimant is the person primarily responsible for Alex, looking after them and making decisions about their upbringing. Alex is not in hospital or local authority care. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are met.

The claimant is the primary person responsible for Alex, who lives with them full-time and is not in hospital or care. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.
